zh.Worcester, Worcestershire, England: 英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾伍斯特
zh.Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England: 英國武斯特斯爾本沃斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Moore於大約1562年出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾本沃斯時，他的父親George Moore28歲，母親Elizabeth Cope26歲。他於1585年11月19日與Ellen Elizabeth Taylor在英國武斯特斯爾本沃斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1585年11月19日在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾伍斯特享年24歲，埋葬在英國武斯特斯爾本沃斯。

zh-hans.Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England: 英国武斯特斯爾本沃斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Moore于大约1562年出生在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾本沃斯时，他的父亲George Moore28岁，母亲Elizabeth Cope26岁。他于1585年11月19日与Ellen Elizabeth Taylor在英国武斯特斯爾本沃斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1585年11月19日在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾伍斯特享年24岁，埋葬在英国武斯特斯爾本沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: French: nickname for a dark-skinned man, from a diminutive of Old French more ‘Moor’ (see More ).

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Moore was born about 1562, in Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, George Moore, was 28 and his mother, Elizabeth Cope, was 26. He married Ellen Elizabeth Taylor on 19 November 1585, in Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died on 19 November 1585, in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 24, and was buried in Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Moore est né environ 1562, à Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, George Moore, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Cope, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Ellen Elizabeth Taylor le 19 novembre 1585, à Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 19 novembre 1585, à Worcester, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 24 ans, et a été enterré à Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Moore um 1562 in Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater George Moore 28 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Cope 26. Er hat am 19. November 1585 in Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England Ellen Elizabeth Taylor geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 19. November 1585 im Alter von 24 Jahren in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Moore è nato il circa 1562 nel Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, George Moore, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Cope, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Ellen Elizabeth Taylor il 19 novembre 1585, nel Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 19 novembre 1585, nel Worcester, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 24 anni, e fu sepolto a Bengeworth, Worcestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Mooreが約1562年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアベンジェウオルスで生まれた時，父George Mooreは28歳，母Elizabeth Copeは26歳であった。1585年11月19日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアベンジェウオルスでEllen Elizabeth Taylorと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1585年11月19日にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアウオルセスターで24歳で死去し，イギリスウオルセステルシュアイアベンジェウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Moore(이)가 대략 1562년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 벤그워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George Moore(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Cope(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1585년 11월 19일 영국 워스스터실르 벤그워스에서 Ellen Elizabeth Taylor(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1585년 11월 19일 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 워스스터에서 24세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워스스터실르 벤그워스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Moore nasceu aproximadamente 1562, em Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, George Moore, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Cope, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Ellen Elizabeth Taylor em 19 de novembro de 1585, em Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 19 de novembro de 1585, em Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 24 anos, e foi sepultado em Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Moore родился около 1562 г. в местности бенгюорт, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени George Moore было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Cope, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Ellen Elizabeth Taylor 19 ноября 1585 г. в местности бенгюорт, ворсестиршир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер 19 ноября 1585 г. в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 24 лет и был похоронен в местности бенгюорт, ворсестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Moore nació aproximadamente 1562, en Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, George Moore, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Cope, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Ellen Elizabeth Taylor el 19 de noviembre de 1585, en Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 19 de noviembre de 1585, en Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 24 años, y fue sepultado en Bengeworth, Worcestershire, Inglaterra.
